channel.title: Every
channel.description: The only subscription you need to stay at the edge of AI. Ideas, apps, and training.

item.title: Vibe Check: GPT-5.4—OpenAI Is Back
item.description: GPT-5.4 is fast, opinionated, and good enough to tempt our Opus loyalist

item.title: Creative Work Is About to Look a Lot More Like Programming
item.description: Flora's Weber Wong on why creative professionals need to stop thinking in artifacts and start thinking in systems

item.title: How Claws Took Over Every
item.description: Plus: Partners at the world’s slowest incubator share their secrets on company building

item.title: You Have a Claw. Now What?
item.description: Our comprehensive guide to OpenClaw is here

item.title: OpenClaw: Setting Up Your First Personal AI Agent
item.description: Demos, workflows, and hard-won lessons from building agents that run 24/7

item.title: You Should Never Go Viral With Your AI App
item.description: In the agent-native era, growth follows different rules

item.title: The Case for Letting Your AI Forget
item.description: Plus: How we use AI in our writing

item.title: How to Design Software With Weight
item.description: A look at the design principles that guided our smart dictation app from desktop to iPhone

item.title: This Is How the Every Editorial Team Uses AI
item.description: We’re publishing our editorial guidelines, plus the workflows our writers, editors, and producers have built around them

item.title: Why I Turned Off ChatGPT’s Memory
item.description: The case for keeping your AI on a need-to-know basis

item.title: What Board Games Taught Me About Working with AI
item.description: The skills I transferred to my writing agent from playing Settlers of Catan

item.title: Vibe Check: Anthropic Just Made Opus Cheaper Without Calling It That
item.description: Sonnet 4.6 delivers Opus-close performance at half the price—but speed didn't come along for the ride

item.title: How to Build Agent-native: Lessons From Four Apps
item.description: Start with three simple tools, and let the AI figure out the rest

item.title: The Two-slice Team
item.description: Amazon’s “two-pizza rule” worked for the past twenty-four years. We need a new heuristic for the next twenty-four.

item.title: Compound Engineering: The Definitive Guide
item.description: A comprehensive handbook for the AI-native engineering philosophy

item.title: Vibe Check: Opus 4.6—The Best Coding Model We’ve Tested (With Some Maddening Habits)
item.description: It one-shotted a problem other models missed—and brings agentic, parallel work to non-coding tasks

item.title: Vibe Check: OpenAI's Codex App Gains Ground on Claude Code
item.description: OpenAI nailed the interface. But it's built for hardcore engineering.

item.title: The Heyday of the Writing-first Practitioner
item.description: How some of the world’s most famous investors and operators stand out—and why AI sharpens this edge

item.title: I Asked Claude the Question I Could Never Ask My Boss
item.description: ‘Does this mean I'm good at my job?’

item.title: How AI Can Cut Your Planning Cycle From Two Weeks to Two Days
item.description: Three simple tools will save you hours—plus, the seven-step process for implementation

item.title: Think First, AI Second
item.description: Three principles for keeping your cognitive edge while leveraging AI's capabilities

item.title: We Tested Claude Sonnet 4.5 for Writing and Editing
item.description: Five tests across blind comparisons, editorial standards, and deadlines—here's what changed our setup
